title: M6.1 hpds audit report-github
description:
hpds audit report-github: consumes audit JSON and via gh with GITHUB_TOKEN: on PRs upsert a single sticky comment (marker <!-- hpds-audit -->) with findings table, update in place; on schedule file one issue per new Error finding, labeled hpds-audit, with stable fingerprint (check_id + path) in a marker comment for idempotent dedup — never duplicate an open issue for the same finding; close resolved ones with a comment. Bot logic lives in hpds itself so the workflow file stays a thin shim.

AC (binding): unit tests for fingerprinting + comment/issue body generation; gh calls behind a trait faked in tests.

See spec §8.3 and §12 M6.1. Spec: scratch/hpds-cli-design.md (absolute: /Users/malcolmbarrett/core/active/hpds-cli/scratch/hpds-cli-design.md — scratch/ is gitignored, NOT present in git worktrees; always read via the absolute path).

title: M5.1 Check framework + Finding + report renderer
description:
Check framework: each check implements fn run(&self, ctx) -> Vec<Finding>; Finding { check_id, severity: Error|Warn|Info, message, remediation }. Report renderer grouped by severity with remediation hints; --format json emits { repo, findings: [...], summary } with a stable schema. Exit 1 if any Error-severity findings, else 0. --strict promotes warnings.

Prefer shelling to git for fidelity.

title: M7.1 hpds git vaccinate
description:
hpds git vaccinate — usethis::git_vaccinate generalized: appends to the GLOBAL git ignore file (core.excludesFile, creating ~/.gitignore and setting the config if unset) the R patterns (.Rhistory, .RData, .Rproj.user, .Rdata, .httr-oauth, .DS_Store) plus curated Python patterns (__pycache__/, *.py[cod], .venv/, .ipynb_checkpoints/, .env, *.egg-info/, .pytest_cache/, .mypy_cache/, .ruff_cache/), and general editor junk. Idempotent (marker block); --project variant appends to the repo's .gitignore instead.

description:
Fetched templates: slides (StanfordHPDS/hpds-slides-theme), poster (StanfordHPDS/hpds-poster), thesis (StanfordHPDS/typst-stanford-thesis). Prefer quarto use template <repo> when quarto is on PATH and repo is a Quarto template (slides, poster); otherwise (or for the typst thesis) shallow-clone via gh repo clone/git clone --depth 1 into the target, strip .git, print next steps. Network required; fail clearly offline.

AC (binding): online-tests fetch each real repo into a temp dir.

See spec §6 and §12 M3.6. Spec: scratch/hpds-cli-design.md (absolute: /Users/malcolmbarrett/core/active/hpds-cli/scratch/hpds-cli-design.md — scratch/ is gitignored, NOT present in git worktrees; always read via the absolute path).

title: M3.7 hpds init wizard + --yes
description:
hpds init (alias hpds project init) interactive wizard: name + description (defaults from dir name), language(s) R/Python/both (renv and/or uv conventions), multi-select components delegating to hpds use (pipeline, readme, container, slurm, gha), offers git init + hpds git vaccinate + optionally hpds repo create at the end, writes hpds.toml with [project] metadata. Non-interactive: hpds init --yes [--language r,python] [--use make,readme,...]. Every interactive flow must have a flag-driven non-interactive equivalent.

AC (binding): integration test of non-interactive path produces complete project; interactive flow tested via inquire's test support or documented manual script for the smoke agent.

See spec §6 and §12 M3.7. Spec: scratch/hpds-cli-design.md (absolute: /Users/malcolmbarrett/core/active/hpds-cli/scratch/hpds-cli-design.md — scratch/ is gitignored, NOT present in git worktrees; always read via the absolute path).
